summary: Backend-only verification completed for wh_5 dominant-fact step resolution fix. All 5 targeted test files pass (90 tests total). The no_12v_supply branch correctly routes negative wh_5 responses to upstream diagnostics (wh_5a/wh_5b/wh_5c) while preserving positive path to wh_6 and clarification behavior.
critical_code_review_comments: wh_5a/wh_5b/wh_5c steps correctly added with RU localizations (lines 170-193) and EN definitions (lines 748-781)
no_12v_supply branch definition correctly triggers from wh_5 with multilingual pattern (EN/RU) for 'no 12V' or '0V' readings
Branch has no mutuallyExclusive conflicts, allowing independent upstream power diagnostics
Total water heater step count correctly updated from 21 to 24 in language-lock tests
3 new regression tests verify: negative→branch, positive→normal, clarification→no-branch
context_for_next_testing_agent: wh_5 dominant-fact routing verified. The no_12v_supply branch (wh_5a→wh_5b→wh_5c) is triggered when wh_5 response indicates no 12V DC at control board. Positive 12V readings continue to wh_6 (ignition check). Clarification requests stay on wh_5 without triggering branch. RU language-lock behavior preserved with updated 24-step total.
rca of the issue: N/A - This was a feature addition, not a bug fix. Implementation correctly adds deterministic branch routing for upstream 12V supply diagnostics when wh_5 indicates no board power.
